channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Oracle Red Bull Racing Team Revs Up Automation to Boost Security
item.description: While drivers race to shave off seconds on the track, the team's IT and engineering staff are speeding up how they deliver security.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: UK: Education Sector Faces Surge in Cyber Breaches Despite Stable National Threat Levels
item.description: The British public education sector has faced the nation’s most dramatic increase in cyber breach prevalence over the past year

item.title: New Python Backdoor Uses Tunneling Service to Steal Browser and Cloud Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a stealthy Python-based backdoor framework called DEEP#DOOR that comes with capabilities to establish persistent access and harvest a wide range of sensitive information from compromised hosts. "The intrusion chain begins with execution of a batch script ('install_obf.bat') that disables Windows security controls, dynamically extracts an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical Gemini CLI Flaw Enabled Host Code Execution, Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: <p>An attacker could have planted a malicious configuration to execute commands outside the sandbox.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/critical-gemini-cli-flaw-enabled-host-code-execution-supply-chain-attacks/">Critical Gemini CLI Flaw Enabled Host Code Execution, Supply Chain Attacks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: EnOcean SmartServer Flaws Expose Buildings to Remote Hacking
item.description: <p>Claroty researchers discovered two vulnerabilities that can be exploited for security bypass and remote code execution.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/enocean-smartserver-flaws-expose-buildings-to-remote-hacking/">EnOcean SmartServer Flaws Expose Buildings to Remote Hacking</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: EtherRAT Distribution Spoofing Administrative Tools via GitHub Facades
item.description: Intro A sophisticated, high-resilience malicious campaign was identified by Atos Threat Research Center (TRC) in March 2026. This operation specifically targets the high-privilege professional accounts of enterprise administrators, DevOps engineers, and security analysts by impersonating administrative utilities they rely on for daily operations. By integrating&nbsp;Search Engine Order (SEO)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical cPanel & WHM Vulnerability Exploited as Zero-Day for Months
item.description: <p>The authentication bypass flaw allows attackers to gain administrative access to vulnerable servers.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/critical-cpanel-whm-vulnerability-exploited-as-zero-day-for-months/">Critical cPanel &#038; WHM Vulnerability Exploited as Zero-Day for Months</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Fast16 Malware
item.description: <p>Researchers have reverse-engineered a piece of malware named Fast16. It&#8217;s almost certainly state-sponsored, probably US in origin, and was <a href="https://www.wired.com/story/fast16-malware-stuxnet-precursor-iran-nuclear-attack/?_sp=72d58355-e351-43ad-ba73-bc2b546a30a0.1777128353268">deployed</a> against Iran years before Stuxnet:</p> <blockquote><p>&#8220;&#8230;the Fast16 malware was designed to carry out the most subtle form of sabotage ever seen in an in-the-wild malware tool: By automatically spreading across networks and then silently manipulating computation processes in certain software applications that perform high-precision mathematical calculations and simulate physical phenomena, Fast16 can alter the results of those programs to cause failures that range from faulty research results to catastrophic damage to real-world equipment.&#8221;...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: ‘Copy Fail’ Logic Flaw in Linux Kernel Enables System Takeover
item.description: <p>Affecting the kernel’s authencesn cryptographic template, the vulnerability was introduced in 2017 and impacts all distributions.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/copy-fail-logic-flaw-in-linux-kernel-enables-system-takeover/">&#8216;Copy Fail&#8217; Logic Flaw in Linux Kernel Enables System Takeover</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Europol Busts Albanian Scam Call Centers in Major Online Fraud Case
item.description: European police arrested 10 suspects after dismantling Albanian scam call centers linked to a €50m ($58m) online investment fraud operation

item.title: New Linux 'Copy Fail' Vulnerability Enables Root Access on Major Distributions
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) flaw that could allow an unprivileged local user to obtain root. The high-severity vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-31431 (CVSS score: 7.8) has been codenamed Copy Fail by Xint.io and Theori. "An unprivileged local user can write four controlled bytes into the page cache of any readable file on a Linux
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cyber is the Number One Global “People Risk,” Says Marsh
item.description: Marsh’s 2026 People Risks survey finds cyber‑related challenges dominate, as cyber‑threat literacy tops risks and cyber and AI skills shortages rise

item.title: Sandhills Medical Says Ransomware Breach Affects 170,000
item.description: <p>It took the healthcare organization nearly one year to publicly disclose a data breach after it was targeted by Inc Ransom.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/sandhills-medical-says-ransomware-breach-affects-170000/">Sandhills Medical Says Ransomware Breach Affects 170,000</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Google Fixes CVSS 10 Gemini CLI CI RCE and Cursor Flaws Enable Code Execution
item.description: Google has addressed a maximum severity security flaw in Gemini CLI -- the "@google/gemini-cli" npm package and the "google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli" GitHub Actions workflow -- that could have allowed attackers to execute arbitrary commands on host systems. "The vulnerability allowed an unprivileged external attacker to force their own malicious content to load as Gemini configuration,"
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Confirms Windows Flaw Is Being Exploited After Incomplete Patch
item.description: <p>Microsoft confirmed a Windows zero-click flaw tied to an incomplete patch is being exploited, putting credentials at risk for unpatched users.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-windows-zero-click-flaw-incomplete-patch/">Microsoft Confirms Windows Flaw Is Being Exploited After Incomplete Patch</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Claude Mythos Fears Startle Japan's Financial Services Sector
item.description: Global financial institutions are panicked over Anthropic's new superhacker AI model. Cyber experts aren't quite as worried.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Reverse Engineering With AI Unearths High-Severity GitHub Bug
item.description: Wiz used an AI reverse-engineering tool to pinpoint a vulnerability that previously would have been too costly and time-consuming to undertake.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Hackers Abuse Robinhood Signup Process to Deliver Phishing Emails
item.description: <p>Robinhood fixed an account-creation flaw that hackers abused to send convincing phishing emails from its own system to some users over the weekend.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-robinhood-phishing-emails-official-address/">Hackers Abuse Robinhood Signup Process to Deliver Phishing Emails</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: AI Finds 38 Security Flaws in Electronic Health Record Platform
item.description: Flaws in OpenEMR's platform — used by more than 100,000 healthcare providers — enabled database compromise, remote code execution, and data theft.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: SAS Launches AI Governance Tools to Tame Agentic AI in the Enterprise
item.description: <p>SAS expands Viya with governed AI agents, copilots, and new governance tools aimed at helping enterprises manage shadow AI and build trust in automation.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-sas-agentic-ai-governance-tools/">SAS Launches AI Governance Tools to Tame Agentic AI in the Enterprise</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Drew Robb

item.title: The Shadow Supply Chain: A Pivot To Usage-Based Discovery | UpGuard
item.description: Pivot from "purchase-based" to usage-based discovery. See how browser telemetry and identity logs work together to secure your true vendor footprint.

item.title: 1 in 15 MCP Servers are Lookalikes: Is Your Org at Risk? | UpGuard
item.description: For every official MCP server, up to 15 lookalikes exist. Learn to identify these registry-layer threats and discover methods to protect your organization.

item.title: The Context Gap: How Nearly Half of Your Time is Lost to Investigation | UpGuard
item.description: Security teams waste 43% of their response time on manual context gathering, making high-context intelligence a necessity in modern cybersecurity.

item.title: MCP: The AI Protocol Quietly Expanding Your Attack Surface | UpGuard
item.description: As AI tools adopt MCP, security teams face a new shadow IT crisis. Learn what MCP is, why these servers carry risk, and how to begin gaining visibility.

item.title: SAP-Related npm Packages Compromised in Credential-Stealing Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about a new supply chain attack campaign targeting SAP-related npm Packages with credential-stealing malware. According to reports from Aikido Security, Onapsis, OX Security,&nbsp;SafeDep, Socket, StepSecurity, and Google-owned Wiz, the campaign – calling itself the mini Shai-Hulud – has affected the following packages associated with SAP's
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Shadow Supply Chain: The SSO & AI Visibility Gap | UpGuard
item.description: Is your SSO enough? 31.4% of vendor interactions bypass identity logs. Discover how Shadow AI and direct logins redefine vendor visibility.

item.title: Vect 2.0 Ransomware Acts as Wiper, Thanks to Design Error
item.description: The emerging ransomware has been deployed against victims of the TeamPCP supply chain attacks, but organizations should think twice before paying for a decryptor.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Cursor Extension Flaw Exposes Developer API Keys
item.description: Cursor flaw lets extensions steal API keys and session tokens without user interaction, according to researchers at LayerX

item.title: New Wave of DPRK Attacks Uses AI-Inserted npm Malware, Fake Firms, and RATs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered malicious code in an npm package after a malicious package as a dependency to the project by Anthropic's Claude Opus large language model (LLM). The package in question is "@validate-sdk/v2," which is listed on npm as a utility software development kit (SDK) for hashing, validation, encoding/decoding, and secure random generation. However, its real
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Malicious npm Dependency Linked to AI Assisted Commit Targets Crypto Wallets
item.description: Researchers uncover a malicious npm dependency linked to an AI‑assisted code commit that steals sensitive data and exposes crypto wallets

item.title: What Is Dark Web Monitoring? | UpGuard
item.description: Understand what dark web monitoring is, and why it’s essential for third-party risk management. Learn how UpGuard helps detect exposed data in real-time.

item.title: Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure
item.description: <p>The vulnerability allows attackers to read data from a LiteLLM proxy’s database and potentially modify it.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/fresh-litellm-vulnerability-exploited-shortly-after-disclosure/">Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Lotus Wiper Attack Targets Venezuelan Energy Firms, Utilities
item.description: An analysis of the destructive malware reveals sophisticated living-off-the-land (LotL) techniques and detailed strategies for the widespread deletion of data.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Researchers Track 2.9 Billion Compromised Credentials
item.description: KELA claims infostealers remained the primary access vector for attacks in 2025

item.title: Hundreds of Internet-Facing VNC Servers Expose ICS/OT
item.description: <p>Forescout has identified tens of thousands of exposed RDP and VNC servers that can be mapped to specific industries.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/hundreds-of-internet-facing-vnc-servers-expose-ics-ot/">Hundreds of Internet-Facing VNC Servers Expose ICS/OT</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
item.description: In February 2026, researchers uncovered a shift that completely changed the game: threat actors are now using custom AI setups to automate attacks directly into the kill chain. We aren't just talking about AI writing better phishing emails anymore. We’re talking about autonomous agents mapping Active Directory and seizing Domain Admin credentials in minutes. The problem? Most defensive workflows
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What to Look for in an Exposure Management Platform (And What Most of Them Get Wrong)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack
item.description: <p>The hackers exfiltrated the data from Checkmarx’s GitHub environment on March 30, a week after publishing malicious code.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/checkmarx-confirms-data-stolen-in-supply-chain-attack/">Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Critical Flaw Turns Vect Ransomware into Data Destroying Wiper
item.description: The Vect 2.0 ransomware wipes large files instead of merely encrypting them, making recovery impossible – even for the attackers

item.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain
item.description: <p>US service members received WhatsApp messages claiming they would be targeted with drones and missiles.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/iranian-cyber-group-handala-targets-us-troops-in-bahrain/">Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Claude Mythos Has Found 271 Zero-Days in Firefox
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: A Quarter of Healthcare Organizations Report Medical Device Cyber-Attacks
item.description: RunSafe report reveals most attacks on medical devices disrupt patient care

item.title: 38 Vulnerabilities Found in OpenEMR Medical Software
item.description: <p>Some of the vulnerabilities discovered by Aisle can be exploited to access and alter sensitive patient information.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/38-vulnerabilities-found-in-openemr-medical-software/">38 Vulnerabilities Found in OpenEMR Medical Software</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
item.description: cPanel has released security updates to address a security issue impacting various authentication paths that could allow an attacker to obtain access to the control panel software. The problem affects all currently supported versions of cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM), according to an alert published by WebPros on Tuesday. It does not have an official identifier. The issue has been addressed in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited ConnectWise and Windows Flaws to KEV
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added two security flaws impacting ConnectWise ScreenConnect and Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2024-1708 (CVSS score: 8.4) - A path traversal vulnerability in&nbsp; ConnectWise ScreenConnect
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 SQL Injection Exploited within 36 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: In yet another instance of threat actors quickly jumping on the exploitation bandwagon, a newly disclosed critical security flaw in BerriAI's LiteLLM Python package has come under active exploitation in the wild within 36 hours of the bug becoming public knowledge. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-42208 (CVSS score: 9.3), is an SQL injection that could be exploited to modify the underlying
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: BlueNoroff Uses Fake Zoom Calls to Turn Victims Into Attack Lures
item.description: The North Korean group is using stolen victim videos, AI-generated avatars, and fake Zoom calls to scale malware attacks against cryptocurrency executives.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: ClickUp Data Leak Exposes Enterprise Emails for Over a Year
item.description: <p>A hardcoded ClickUp API key exposed hundreds of corporate and government emails for over a year, raising new SaaS security concerns.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-clickup-api-key-email-exposure/">ClickUp Data Leak Exposes Enterprise Emails for Over a Year</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: NSA Chief During Snowden Affair Shares Regrets, Reflections 13 Years Later
item.description: Chris Inglis was the head civilian in charge at the NSA when the Snowden leaks exploded. He gets candid about mistakes the organization made, and what CISOs need to know about spotting potential threats, media disclosures, and &quot;enculturation.&quot;
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Staff

item.title: Feuding Ransomware Groups Leak Each Other's Data
item.description: When 0APT and KryBit attacked each other, they exposed infrastructure and operational data, giving defenders rare insight into ransomware operations.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Vidar Rises to Top of Chaotic Infostealer Market
item.description: The malware has filled the gap created by last year's law enforcement takedowns of Lumma and Rhadamanthys.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Researchers Discover Critical GitHub CVE-2026-3854 RCE Flaw Exploitable via Single Git Push
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a critical security vulnerability impacting GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server that could allow an authenticated user to obtain remote code execution with a single "git push" command. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-3854 (CVSS score: 8.7), is a case of command injection that could allow an attacker with push access to a repository to achieve
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Brazilian LofyGang Resurfaces After Three Years With Minecraft LofyStealer Campaign
item.description: A cybercrime group of Brazilian origin has resurfaced after more than three years to orchestrate a campaign that targets Minecraft players with a new stealer called LofyStealer (aka GrabBot). "The malware disguises itself as a Minecraft hack called 'Slinky,'" Brazil-based cybersecurity company ZenoX said in a technical report. "It uses the official game icon to induce voluntary execution,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ADT Confirms Major Data Breach Exposing Millions of Names, Partial SSNs
item.description: <p>ADT confirmed a data breach exposing customer names, addresses, phone numbers, and partial SSNs, with millions of records reportedly affected.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-adt-data-breach-millions-customer-records/">ADT Confirms Major Data Breach Exposing Millions of Names, Partial SSNs</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: Stronger Together – The Value of Participating with PCI SSC
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Medtronic Confirms Data Breach After ShinyHunters Claims
item.description: Medtronic confirms IT breach as ShinyHunters claims millions of records accesseda

item.title: Fresh Wave of GlassWorm VS Code Extensions Slices Through Supply Chain
item.description: Attackers continue to scale a campaign to seed Open VSX with seemingly benign VS Code extensions that spread self-propagating malware.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: VECT 2.0 Ransomware Irreversibly Destroys Files Over 131KB on Windows, Linux, ESXi
item.description: Threat hunters are warning that the cybercriminal operation known as VECT 2.0 acts more like a wiper than a ransomware due to a critical flaw in its encryption implementation across Windows, Linux, and ESXi variants that renders recovery impossible even for the threat actors. The fact that VECT's locker permanently destroys large files rather than encrypting them means even victims who opt to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ransomware Turf War as 0APT and KryBit Groups Trade Blows
item.description: Ransomware groups 0APT and KryBit have doxxed each other online

item.title: Chinese National Extradited Over Silk Typhoon Cyber Campaign
item.description: Extradition links alleged MSS-directed hacker to Silk Typhoon and COVID-19 espionage

item.title: From DMV to Wallet: Understanding Verifiable Digital Credential Issuance
item.description: In our last post in this series, we compared two credential formats that shape the digital identity ecosystem: ISO/IEC 18013-5 and -7 mobile documents (mdocs) and W3C Verifiable Credentials (VCs). Both formats define how a credential is structured and shared, but neither can function without an issuance process. This blog post explores what it takes to issue verifiable digital credentials, with a focus on mobile driver’s licenses (mDLs). We’ll look at how issuance works today in practice, where inconsistencies exist, and how standards bodies (FIDO, ISO and OpenID Foundation) are working to
item.dc:creator: Bill Fisher, Ryan Galluzzo, Heather Flanagan

item.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
item.description: Every security program is betting on the same assumption: once a system is connected, the problem is solved. Open a ticket, stand up a gateway, push the data through. Done. That assumption is wrong. It is also a major reason Zero Trust programs stall. New research my team just published puts numbers on it. The Cyber360: Defending the Digital Battlespace report, based on a survey of 500 security
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical Unpatched Flaw Leaves Hugging Face LeRobot Open to Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a critical security flaw impacting LeRobot, Hugging Face's open-source robotics platform with nearly 24,000 GitHub stars, that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-25874 (CVSS score: 9.3), which has been described as a case of untrusted data deserialization stemming from the use of the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What Anthropic’s Mythos Means for the Future of Cybersecurity
item.description: <p>Two weeks ago, Anthropic <a href="https://red.anthropic.com/2026/mythos-preview/">announced</a> that its new model, Claude Mythos Preview, can autonomously find and weaponize software vulnerabilities, turning them into working exploits without expert guidance. These were vulnerabilities in key software like operating systems and internet infrastructure that thousands of software developers working on those systems failed to find. This capability will have major security implications, compromising the devices and services we use every day. As a result, <a href="https://spectrum.ieee.org/tag/anthropic">Anthropic</a> is not releasing the model to the general public, but instead to a ...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: After Mythos: New Playbooks For a Zero-Window Era
item.description: When patching isn’t fast enough, NDR helps contain the next era of threats. If you’ve been tracking advancements in AI, you know the exploit window, the short buffer that organizations relied on to patch and protect after a vulnerability disclosure, is closing fast. Anthropic’s new model, Claude Mythos, and its Project Glasswing, showed that finding exploitable vulnerabilities and subtle cracks
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Proofpoint Research Reveals Half of Global Organizations Experienced AI Incidents Despite Having AI Security Controls in Place
No Metrics Are Better Than Bad Metrics in the SOC, Says NCSC
item.description: The National Cyber Security Centre has warned against measuring SOCs with ticket-based metrics

item.title: North Korean Hackers Target Crypto Firms with ClickFix and AI-Made Zoom Lures
item.description: Arctic Wolf attributed this large-scale spear-phishing campaign to BlueNoroff, a financially motivated subgroup of the Lazarus Group

item.title: Chinese Silk Typhoon Hacker Extradited to U.S. Over COVID Research Cyberattacks
item.description: A Chinese national accused of being a member of the Silk Typhoon hacking group has been extradited to the U.S. from Italy.&nbsp; Xu Zewei, 34, was arrested in July 2025 by Italian authorities for his alleged links to the Chinese state-sponsored threat group and for orchestrating cyber attacks against American organizations and government agencies between February 2020 and June 2021, including
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
item.description: An administrative role meant for artificial intelligence (AI) agents within Microsoft Entra ID could enable privilege escalation and identity takeover attacks, according to new findings from Silverfort. Agent ID Administrator is a privileged built-in role introduced by Microsoft as part of its agent identity platform to handle all aspects of an AI agent's identity lifecycle operations in a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Confirms Active Exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202
item.description: Microsoft on Monday revised its advisory for a now-patched, high-severity security flaw impacting Windows Shell to acknowledge that it has been actively exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-32202 (CVSS score: 4.3), a spoofing vulnerability that could allow an attacker to access sensitive information. It was addressed as part of its Patch Tuesday update for this
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UNC6692 Combines Social Engineering, Malware, Cloud Abuse
item.description: A newly discovered threat actor is using Microsoft Teams, AWS S3 buckets, and custom &quot;Snow&quot; malware in a multipronged campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Unpatched 'PhantomRPC' Flaw in Windows Enables Privilege Escalation
item.description: A researcher discovered five different exploit paths that stem from an architectural weakness in how Windows' Remote Procedure Call (RPC) mechanism handles connections to unavailable services.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Dreamplug Technologies Private Limited (CRED)
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: US Sanctions Target Cambodian Scam Network Leaders
item.description: US sanctions target Cambodian scam networks tied to crypto fraud and trafficking

item.title: Checkmarx Confirms GitHub Repository Data Posted on Dark Web After March 23 Attack
item.description: Checkmarx has disclosed that its ongoing investigation tied to the supply chain security incident has revealed that a cybercriminal group published data related to the company on the dark web. "Based on current evidence, we believe this data originated from Checkmarx's GitHub repository, and that access to that repository was facilitated through the initial supply chain attack of March 23, 2026,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Utilities Tech Supplier Itron Discloses Cyber-Attack, Operations Unaffected
item.description: Itron confirmed a cyber incident but does not believe it is likely to have a material impact on the company

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fast16 Malware, XChat Launch, Federal Backdoor, AI Employee Tracking & More
item.description: Everything is dumb again. This week feels broken in a very familiar way. Old tricks are back. New tools are doing shady crap. Supply chains got hit. Fake help desks worked. Weird research showed how easy some attacks still are. Most of it feels like stuff we should have fixed years ago. Bad extensions. Stolen creds. Remote tools are getting abused. Malware hides in places people trust. Same
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Widely Used Browser Extensions Selling User Data
item.description: Dozens of browser extensions openly sell user data via privacy policy disclosures

item.title: 20-Year-Old Malware Rewrites History of Cyber Sabotage
item.description: Researchers have uncovered a malware framework dubbed &quot;fast16&quot; that predates Stuxnet by five years.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Parsing Agentic Offensive Security's Existential Threat
item.description: Some fear frontier LLMs like Claude Mythos and OpenAI's GPT-5.5 will lead to cybersecurity annihilation. Ari Herbert-Voss notes this could be an opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Tara Seals

item.title: Mythos Changed the Math on Vulnerability Discovery. Most Teams Aren't Ready for the Remediation Side
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude Mythos Preview has dominated security discussions since its April 7 announcement. Early reporting describes a powerful cybersecurity-focused AI system capable of identifying vulnerabilities at scale and raising serious questions about how quickly organizations can validate, prioritize, and remediate what it finds. The debate that followed has mostly focused on the right
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: PhantomCore Exploits TrueConf Vulnerabilities to Breach Russian Networks
item.description: A pro-Ukrainian hacktivist group called PhantomCore has been attributed to attacks actively targeting servers running TrueConf video conferencing software in Russia since September 2025. That's according to a report published by Positive Technologies, which found the threat actors to be leveraging an exploit chain comprising three vulnerabilities to execute commands remotely on susceptible
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Most Cybersecurity Professionals Feel Undervalued and Underpaid
item.description: A new report by global technology recruitment firm, Harvey Nash, found that three quarters of cybersecurity staff are pessimistic on pay and half are looking for a new job

item.title: Researchers Uncover 73 Fake VS Code Extensions Delivering GlassWorm v2 Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged dozens of Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extensions on the Open VSX repository that are linked to a persistent information-stealing campaign dubbed GlassWorm. The cluster of 73 extensions has been identified as cloned versions of their legitimate counterparts. Of these, six have been confirmed to be malicious, with the remaining acting as seemingly
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Medieval Encrypted Letter Decoded
item.description: <p>Sent by a Spanish diplomat. Apparently people have been <a href="https://www.medievalists.net/2026/04/secret-letter-detailing-late-medieval-britain-fully-decoded/">working on it</a> since it was rediscovered in 1860.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Researchers Identify Fast16 Sabotage Malware That Pre-Dates Stuxnet
item.description: The “fast16” malware may have been used to target Iran’s nuclear program prior to Stuxnet

item.title: BlackFile Group Targets Retail and Hospitality with Vishing Attacks
item.description: Researchers uncover a new data theft and extortion group dubbed “BlackFile”

item.title: Fake CAPTCHA IRSF Scam and 120 Keitaro Campaigns Drive Global SMS, Crypto Fraud
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a telecommunications fraud campaign that uses fake CAPTCHA verification tricks to dupe unsuspecting users into sending international text messages that incur charges on their mobile bills, generating illicit revenue for the threat actors who lease the phone numbers. According to a new report published by Infoblox, the operation is believed to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Researchers Uncover Pre-Stuxnet ‘fast16’ Malware Targeting Engineering Software
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new Lua-based malware created years before the notorious Stuxnet worm that aimed to sabotage Iran's nuclear program by destroying uranium enrichment centrifuges. According to a new report published by SentinelOne, the previously undocumented cyber sabotage framework dates back to 2005, primarily targeting high-precision calculation software to tamper
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds 4 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets May 2026 Federal Deadline
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added four vulnerabilities impacting SimpleHelp, Samsung MagicINFO 9 Server, and D-Link DIR-823X series routers to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The list of vulnerabilities is below -
CVE-2024-57726 (CVSS score: 9.9) - A missing authorization vulnerability in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Helping Romance Scam Victims Requires a Proactive, Empathic Approach
item.description: People targeted by confidence schemes find getting help is a lonely road. Experts want law enforcement, financial, and government institutions to work together and protect them.
item.dc:creator: Bree Fowler

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: How Squid Survived Extinction Events
item.description: <p>Science <a href="https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/03/260331001100.htm">news</a>:</p> <blockquote><p>Scientists have finally cracked a long-standing mystery about squid and cuttlefish evolution by analyzing newly sequenced genomes alongside global datasets. The research reveals that these bizarre, intelligent creatures likely originated deep in the ocean over 100 million years ago, surviving mass extinction events by retreating into oxygen-rich deep-sea refuges. For millions of years, their evolution barely changed&#8212;until a dramatic post-extinction boom sparked rapid diversification as they moved into new shallow-water habitats. ...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: FIRESTARTER Backdoor Hit Federal Cisco Firepower Device, Survives Security Patches
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has revealed that an unnamed federal civilian agency's Cisco Firepower device running Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA) software was compromised in September 2025 with a new malware called FIRESTARTER. FIRESTARTER, per CISA and the U.K.'s National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC), is assessed to be a backdoor designed for remote access
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: US Busts Myanmar Ring Targeting US Citizens in Financial Fraud
item.description: Some 29 people were charged, including a Cambodian senator, and authorities seized more than 500 Web domains tied to fake investment sites.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Glasswing Secured the Code. The Rest of Your Stack Is Still on You
item.description: Forgotten integrations, shadow IT, SaaS, and now shadow AI and agents are everywhere, and attackers don't need sophisticated AI models to take advantage.
item.dc:creator: Ron Peled

item.title: NASA Employees Duped in Chinese Phishing Scheme Targeting U.S. Defense Software
item.description: The Office of Inspector General (OIG) of the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) has revealed how a Chinese national posed as a U.S. researcher as part of a spear-phishing campaign to obtain sensitive information from the space agency, as well as from government entities, universities, and private companies, in violation of export control laws. "For years, NASA employees
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Corporate Shakeups Define the Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from April 20–24.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-upgrades-security-breaches-and-corporate-shakeups-define-the-week-in-tech/">AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Corporate Shakeups Define the Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: AI Phishing Is No. 1 With a Bullet for Cyberattackers
item.description: In the past six months, companies have seen a significant influx of AI-powered phishing, as cyberattackers progress from small campaigns to 1-to-1 personalized attacks.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: UK Biobank Data Breach: Health Data of 500,000 Listed for Sale in China
item.description: UK government Minister confirms that breached health records of UK Biobank volunteers were up for sale on Chinese ecommerce platforms before being removed
